author: bridge-bot
content: <gakonst> What's the game plan for cracking up trie / state size in the perfnets?

author: bridge-bot
content:
<gakonst> re: **@gakonst**
> What's the game plan for cracking up trie / state size in the perfnets?
@KamilChNethermind what if you guys did a Genesis file with a 0x, 1x, 2x, 5x, 10x state size of mainnet and tested with it? Given I/O matters a lot.

author: bridge-bot
content:
<FunnyGiulio> re: **@gakonst**
> @KamilChNethermind what if you guys did a Genesis file with a 0x, 1x, 2x, 5x, 10x state size of mainnet and tested with it? Given I/O matters a lot.
Genesis file is hard. Format is not friendly when big. Mainnet is 30% accs 70% storage. So it is important we balance it too. I think you need a new format to import big state tho

author: bridge-bot
content: <sophiagold0> I think there was concern some clients can’t handle configs that large so they’re trying to use a single node to generate a chain we can shadow fork from

author: bridge-bot
content:
<gakonst> re: **@FunnyGiulio**
> Genesis file is hard. Format is not friendly when big. Mainnet is 30% accs 70% storage. So it is important we balance it too. I think you need a new format to import big state tho
Gotcha. That seems like a simple thing to do given importance. Agree on the 30-70 split.

content:

> I think there was concern some clients can’t handle configs that large so they’re trying to use a single node to generate a chain we can shadow fork from

author: bridge-bot
content: <gakonst> Just ship a realistic bench first and we can make running the bench easier after it's validated

author: bridge-bot
content: <gakonst> The existing work is really great to be clear

author: bridge-bot
content:
<notnotstorm_p> re: **@gakonst**
> Dont think that matters, do 5 files
sure do whatever is path of least resistance. but if this is new functionality that each client has to implement then might as well make them consume the same format

OTOH if it's just snapshotting the underlying db files of each client, then no new functionality needed. but then the initial generation of the state might become more difficult

content:

author: bridge-bot
content: <FunnyGiulio> Maybe not so hard to just create blocks from genesis and import huge giant chain

author: bridge-bot
content: <ben_a_adams> Is size the problem or structure (deep tries); can create worst case structure to test without it needing to be terrabytes; and if just go for raw size might not cover the problem areas (change amplification on roots etc)

author: bridge-bot
content:
<gakonst> re: **@ben_a_adams**
> Is size the problem or structure (deep tries); can create worst case structure to test without it needing to be terrabytes; and if just go for raw size might not cover the problem areas (change amplification on roots etc)
Deep trie causes IO amplification, which hurts read perf (bc random io) and increases mem required for batch writes when persisting the trie. Size itself not really an issue.

author: bridge-bot
content: <gakonst> More impt the read perf

author: bridge-bot
content:
<ben_a_adams> So could probably do something with a genesis file and it doesn't necessarily have to be huge (though it may be large). Might have advantage on chain resets than snapshots; though not sure.

Also construction rather than having a public runnable sequence of tx on how to ruin a chain 😅

author: bridge-bot
content: <ben_a_adams> Or could just copy Xen token' storage , but would be a 20GB+ json file

author: bridge-bot
content:
<amezianehamlat> How about the initial idea of spamming with storage txs, with 1 gigas blocks and 4 s slot time, we can go ~80 (1000/36 * 3) times faster than current mainnet. In one month, this will generate ~ 6 years mainnet activity, in two months ~12 years which should be sufficient. This is only by having similar block structure than current mainnet, so we can go even faster with blocks with more storage txs.
The other benefit is to stress storage opcodes.

author: bridge-bot
content:
<gakonst> re: **@amezianehamlat**
> How about the initial idea of spamming with storage txs, with 1 gigas blocks and 4 s slot time, we can go ~80 (1000/36 * 3) times faster than current mainnet. In one month, this will generate ~ 6 years mainnet activity, in two months ~12 years which should be sufficient. This is only by having similar block structure than current mainnet, so we can go even faster with blocks with more storage txs.
The other benefit is to stress storage opcodes.
Idts, if you 10-100x mainnet gas this replay method isn't good. Just generate an iterable dataset and bootstrap node dbs from it.

author: bridge-bot
content: <amezianehamlat> Yeah, it is not easy to replay if needed but can be a good first approach while thinking about a better way to do it.

author: bridge-bot
content:
<KamilChNethermind> 10-100 is great but 2x for now will give us a lot of answers and we can iterate further.

Will do my Best increase the size or will considered shadowforking mainnet and building on top of it
